questions.text: A single Purchase Order (PO) is used for both the sorting/rework execution and the service fee in the new NEO system.
The "Available to Promise" (ATP) function in SAP helps determine if the required delivery quantity of materials can be met by the requested delivery date.
Movement type 542 is always automated in the “Return of Components/BOM Headers” process within NEO.
A NEO SAP Contract represents a legal agreement for L'Oréal to purchase materials from a specific vendor at a defined price, for a specific period and/or target quantity.
The unpacking process only applies to activities performed internally at the L’Oréal Distribution Center (DC).
If a subcontractor or 3PL performs the unpacking, a purchase order is required to cover the unpacking service costs.
The "Return of Components/BOM Headers" process covers the return of components only when they are stored at the co-packer, not directly from the co-packer.
In the unpacking process, the ASN (Advanced Shipping Notice) from the co-packer initiates the corresponding movements in the SAP system.
Sorting and Rework operations are always planned and scheduled in advance within the NEO system.
In the new way of working with NEO, it is not required to create a specific inspection type.
